objective: Review the agent-platform metadata hardening changes for drift, coverage, and maintainability risk.
summary: The review focused on whether the new metadata files, refresh tooling, and docs stay aligned and remain explicit about current platform limits.
assumptions: The repository continues to use the single-entry orchestrator as the primary workflow surface.

evidence.summary: The repo map, skill registry, and run-artifact schema were read together to verify topology, trigger coverage, and artifact structure remained consistent.
The runbook was read after edits to confirm the new metadata layer and validation commands were documented for contributors.

taskLedger.notes: Repo map, skill registry, and run-artifact schema were read together during the breadth scan.
The working context was trimmed to metadata plus runbook evidence before final review output.

verification.details: Metadata and docs were checked together to confirm the workflow layer, repo topology, and skill inventory stayed aligned.
Unit tests for the sync tool still require a local pytest run.

residualRisks: Deterministic metadata still depends on contributors running the sync check during local verification.
